page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Graphics processing is handled by a single NVIDIA Tesla P4 GPU with 8 GB of Pascal-architecture VRAM. This instance does not include local storage and relies on network-attached storage, operating with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the CPU and memory subsystems operate mostly in the lower tiers compared to modern hardware. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance is in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for cached small-block reads at 340.09 GB/sec, but large-block uncached reads and writes drop to the bottom 25% at 37.18 GB/sec and 25.34 GB/sec respectively. PassMark CPU testing yields an average score of 6698.53, though extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests remain in the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its reliance on older Intel Broadwell CPU architecture and Pascal-generation GPU hardware, which limits its raw computational throughput in modern CPU-heavy tasks. This is reflected in weak database operations and static web serving benchmarks. From a resource-density perspective, the server offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for legacy workloads, providing a dedicated GPU without the premium associated with newer architecture classes. This server is configured for entry-level GPU workloads, legacy CUDA applications, basic machine learning inference, and video transcoding where high-performance CPU throughput is not the primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. It incorporates a single NVIDIA Tesla P4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM, utilizing the Pascal architecture. The instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest CPU and memory performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the bottom 10% and multi-core results in the bottom 25%. Cached small-block memory reads show average performance, but uncached large-block operations and database benchmarks are weak. Given these hardware characteristics, this instance is suitable for entry-level GPU-accelerated tasks, legacy CUDA applications, and basic video transcoding where high CPU performance is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c8g1.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Tesla P4 GPU with 8 GB VRAM, designed for entry-level parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c8g1.2xlarge features 8 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for entry-level GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with an NVIDIA Tesla P4 GPU for entry-level parallel computing tasks.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
1x NVIDIA Tesla P4 GPU with 8 GB Pascal VRAM
32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
No local storage with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for legacy CUDA applications and basic video transcoding
